ocr.text: In the current landscape of educational financing, understanding student loan default rates is increasingly critical for
stakeholders in the educational sector. The data presented in the accompanying table highlights key financial metrics that
correlate with these default rates, offering insights into how institutions manage their loan portfolios. For instance, the total
revenue generated by Chegg Inc.
StudentLoanDefaultRates  ByRegion  ByDemographics
Region  Rate(%)  TotalDefaults  TotalLoans
Europe  5.2  12,000  230,000  NorthAmerica  6.1
TotalRevenue(CHF)  NetIncome(CHF)  CAPEX(CHF)  OperatingIncome(CHF)  LoanPortfolio(CHF)  DefaultRateChange(%)  RevenueGrowth(%)  NetMargin(%)  5,000,000
was CHF 1,000,000, while the company achieved a net income of CHF 250,000, reflecting a net margin of 6.4%. This
profitability is essential for sustaining educational programs and financial aid initiatives. Additionally, the revenue growth
percentages indicate a positive trend over the reporting periods, with growth rates of 5.1%, 4.3%, and 3.8%, respectively. Such
growth can be indicative of an institution's ability to adapt to market demands and support students effectively. Furthermore, the
loan portfolio under management totaled CHF 5,000,000, with specific allocations resulting in defaults that necessitate careful
monitoring. Notably, the default rate change percentage is critical in assessing the financial health of student loans. With a focus
on maintaining a robust financial framework, Chegg Inc. has also earmarked CHF 60,000 and CHF 80,000 for capital
expenditure, underscoring its commitment to enhancing educational technology and resources. This financial overview not only
illustrates the operational capacity of Chegg Inc. but also emphasizes the importance of managing default rates to ensure
sustainable growth and educational accessibility.
